findings.requirement: US-21: First tick — rate columns show 0; delta starts from second tick
findings.detail: Internal contradiction between Architecture section and Testing Strategy. Architecture states: 'ProcPidStat{} zero value produces sValue(0, curr, itv, ticks) which gives the current absolute rate from process start divided by itv — acceptable approximation for first tick.' This is NOT zero. Testing Strategy asserts a unit test 'first tick (empty prev map) → rate columns are 0'. An agent following Architecture will produce non-zero first-tick rates, breaking the unit test and the user-spec acceptance criterion. The two descriptions must be reconciled: either the first-tick behaviour is clamped to zero (return 0 when prev is missing) or the test expectation and user-spec are updated to document the approximation.

findings.requirement: Ticks (jiffies/s) value source for all CPU formulas
findings.detail: The tech-spec uses 'ticks' as a parameter in every CPU formula (formatCPUTime, sValue for CPU %, all_total,s) but never specifies where the value comes from. On Linux this is CLK_TCK (typically 100), obtainable via syscall.Getconf("CLK_TCK") or os/exec sysconf. Hardcoding 100 is common but incorrect on non-standard kernels. Without specifying the source, two agents may choose differently and produce different results. This is a fundamental input to the feature's core calculation.

findings.requirement: US-2/US-3: Rate computation guarded against zero interval
findings.detail: The tech-spec passes 'itv' (elapsed time between ticks) to sValue() for all rate columns but does not specify what happens when itv=0 (e.g., two consecutive ticks in the same wall-clock second, or test setup). Division by zero in sValue() would panic. The tech-spec does not document whether sValue() already guards this, or whether the caller must check. For an L-size feature that performs arithmetic on every tick, this is a required guard that should be explicit in the spec.

The tech-spec describes parsing /proc/[pid]/stat by finding the last ')' and splitting the remainder, but does not specify error handling for malformed content: truncated lines, unexpected field count, non-numeric values in utime/stime positions. The only error path documented is 'file missing → skip row'. A malformed-but-present /proc/[pid]/stat line (e.g., kernel bug, container environment) could cause strconv.ParseFloat panic or silent wrong values. For an L-size feature reading untrusted kernel data, parse error handling must be specified.

findings.requirement: US-20: No horizontal scrolling; min 200-char terminal; query column truncated first
findings.detail: The user-spec explicitly defines the layout constraint: all 17 columns fit without horizontal scrolling on terminals >=200 chars wide, and 'query' is the first column to be truncated on narrower terminals. The tech-spec view definition has 'ColsWidth: map[int]int{}' (empty) and provides no column width assignments or truncation priority. An agent implementing from this spec has no basis for column sizing and would need to guess, risking a screen that overflows or truncates numeric columns instead of query.

findings.requirement: US-18: Aux processes (checkpointer, bgwriter, WAL writer, archiver) not shown
findings.detail: The user-spec calls this out as an explicit constraint with a rationale. The tech-spec does not document this constraint anywhere. It is implicitly satisfied (aux processes have no row in pg_stat_activity so they never enter the pipeline) but a reader of the tech-spec alone would not know this is a deliberate, documented decision. Low risk to implementation correctness, but the omission means this rationale is invisible to reviewers.

summary: 18/23 requirements fully covered, 2 partial, 1 missing. 2 critical findings: (1) internal contradiction on first-tick rate behaviour — Architecture says non-zero approximation, Testing Strategy says zero, user-spec says zero — one will be wrong; (2) ticks (CLK_TCK) source is never specified despite being used in every CPU formula. 3 major findings: itv=0 guard unspecified, procfs parse error handling unspecified, column widths absent for a 17-column screen. 1 minor: aux-process exclusion rationale not carried into tech-spec.
